news.t: Love your stock-index fund? It might be time to spice up your investment strategy.
How a more-focused growth index can cut your risk in the stock market
The S&P 500 is too tech-heavy. This alternative ETF keeps beating it.
7 Great Growth ETFs to Get Your Portfolio Going
Invesco S&P 500 Pure Growth ETF Among Today's US Growth Smart Beta ETFs
Analysts See 10% Gains Ahead For The Invesco S&P 500 Pure Growth ETF

holdings.infoBox: RPG is an equity ETF with a total of 68 individual holdings. The top holdings are Sandisk stock at 9.59%, Micron Technology at 3.91%, Comfort Systems USA at 3.41%, KLA Corporation at 2.55%, and Advanced Micro Devices at 2.44%.

overview.description: The Invesco S&P 500 Pure Growth ETF (RPG) is an exchange-traded fund that is based on the S&P 500 Pure Growth index. The fund tracks an index of primarily large- and mid-caps with strong growth characteristics. The index selects companies from the S&P 500 Index, based on three growth factors. RPG was launched on Mar 1, 2006 and is issued by Invesco.

data.title: Love your stock-index fund? It might be time to spice up your investment strategy.
data.text: There are easy and inexpensive ways to broaden your stock market exposure, even within the S&P 500.

data.title: How a more-focused growth index can cut your risk in the stock market
